row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of $716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B USD. The top two companies hold a significant revenue advantage over the third-place UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) at $269.4B USD.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of $132.2B, a significant $30.3B ahead of NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. The top 3 companies (GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) collectively account for over 60% of the total Net Income among the listed companies.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of $1T from the second-largest Bank of America Corporation (BAC). This dominance underscores JPM's substantial market presence and financial muscle.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) maintains its position as the leader in Total Equity, boasting a staggering 415.3B USD. Notably, the top two companies, GOOGL and AMZN, hold a combined 826.4B USD in Total Equity, a 43% lead over the third-ranked JPM.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, dwarfing the next closest competitor, Bank of America Corporation (BAC), by a significant $1T. This substantial gap underscores the enormous scale and financial complexity of JPM's operations.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation's (BAC) $239.3B. This 43% difference highlights the substantial liquidity advantage JPM holds over its banking peers.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a gross profit of $360.5B USD, a significant gap of over $120B USD from the second-placed Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) with $240.3B USD in gross profit.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with an Operating Income Ebit of 133.1B USD, showcasing a significant gap of 48.7B USD over the second-place NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). The top three companies, AAPL, NVDA, and GOOGL, collectively account for over 60% of the total Operating Income Ebit.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the market with combined share of 43.1% of total revenue, indicating a high degree of concentration in these sectors. Consumer Cyclical's 22.0% share is the largest single sector contribution.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the asset landscape with 64.1% market share, nearly 6 times larger than the next closest sector, Technology. This concentration underscores the sector's significant influence on global economic stability.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology and Communication Services sectors dominate the net income landscape, accounting for 64% of total net income with Technology leading at 39.2%. This significant concentration highlights the outsized influence of these sectors on overall profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA outperforms with a 55.6% net margin, while UNH lags at 2.7%, indicating significant differences in profitability efficiency among these companies.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley's (MA) 193.5% ROE stands out for its exceptional return generation, while JPMorgan Chase's (JPM) 11.21x Debt/Equity ratio indicates significant leverage risk.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet's (GOOGL) 12-month cumulative return of +102.0% stands out as the strongest among the highest-revenue companies. Conversely, Adobe (ADBE) has underperformed with a return of -41.9%, marking the largest decline. These divergent performances reflect the varying fortunes of tech and consumer staples sectors.
